content.json:
# XFF Demo — Proxy (VIP) → App Gateway → APIM → App Service

This workbook demonstrates the **X-Forwarded-For (XFF) problem** when a proxy/VIP sits in front of Application Gateway and API Management.

**Architecture:** `Client → Proxy (VIP) → Application Gateway → APIM → App Service`

| Log Table | IP Field | Shows | Real Client IP? |
|-----------|----------|-------|-----------------|
| `AzureDiagnostics` (App GW) | `clientIP_s` | Proxy VIP IP | **No** |
| `ApiManagementGatewayLogs` | `CallerIpAddress` | App GW IP or Proxy IP | **No** |
| `AppServiceHTTPLogs` | `CIp` | App Gateway / APIM IP | **No** |
| `Application Insights` | `customDimensions["X-Forwarded-For"]` | Full XFF chain | **Yes** (first IP in chain) |

---

---
## 1. App Gateway — What Does `clientIP_s` Show?

> **Key Point:** `clientIP_s` always shows the **TCP peer IP** — which is the Proxy VIP, not the real end user.

content.query:
AzureDiagnostics
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where ResourceType == 'APPLICATIONGATEWAYS'
| summarize RequestCount = count() by clientIP_s
| order by RequestCount desc
| extend Label = case(
    clientIP_s == '4.174.181.251', strcat(clientIP_s, ' ← PROXY VIP'),
    clientIP_s == '4.205.85.213', strcat(clientIP_s, ' ← SELF (health probe)'),
    strcat(clientIP_s, ' ← Direct caller / scanner'))
| project Label, RequestCount
content.title: App Gateway — ClientIP Breakdown (clientIP_s = TCP peer, NOT real client)

content.query:
AzureDiagnostics
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where ResourceType == 'APPLICATIONGATEWAYS'
| summarize RequestCount = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), Source = case(
    clientIP_s == '4.174.181.251', 'Via Proxy (VIP)',
    clientIP_s == '4.205.85.213', 'Self (Health Probe)',
    'Direct / External'
)
| render timechart
content.title: App Gateway — Traffic by Source Over Time

content.query:
AzureDiagnostics
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where ResourceType == 'APPLICATIONGATEWAYS'
| where userAgent_s has 'PowerShell'
| project TimeGenerated, clientIP_s, requestUri_s, httpStatus_d, userAgent_s
| extend Source = case(
    clientIP_s == '4.174.181.251', '🔶 Proxy VIP',
    '🟢 Direct (your real IP)'
)
| project TimeGenerated, Source, clientIP_s, httpStatus_d, requestUri_s
| order by TimeGenerated desc
content.title: App Gateway — Test Requests (PowerShell) — Compare Direct vs Proxied

content.json:
---
## 2. App Service — What Does `CIp` Show?

> **Key Point:** `AppServiceHTTPLogs.CIp` always shows the **App Gateway IP**, not the real client. This is the behavior the user reported.

content.query:
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize RequestCount = count() by CIp
| order by RequestCount desc
| extend Label = case(
    CIp == '4.205.85.213', strcat(CIp, ' ← APP GATEWAY IP (not real client!)'),
    strcat(CIp, ' ← Direct caller'))
| project Label, RequestCount
content.title: App Service — CIp Breakdown (CIp = App Gateway IP, NOT real client)

content.query:
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize RequestCount = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), ScStatus = tostring(ScStatus)
| render timechart
content.title: App Service — HTTP Traffic by Status Code

content.json:
---
## 3. APIM — What Does `CallerIpAddress` Show?

> **Key Point:** `ApiManagementGatewayLogs.CallerIpAddress` shows the **TCP peer IP** — which is the App Gateway or Proxy IP, not the real end user. XFF headers must be inspected to find the real client.

content.query:
ApiManagementGatewayLogs
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize RequestCount = count() by CallerIpAddress
| order by RequestCount desc
| extend Label = case(
    CallerIpAddress == '4.174.181.251', strcat(CallerIpAddress, ' ← PROXY VIP'),
    CallerIpAddress == '4.205.85.213', strcat(CallerIpAddress, ' ← APP GATEWAY'),
    strcat(CallerIpAddress, ' ← Direct caller'))
| project Label, RequestCount
content.title: APIM — CallerIpAddress Breakdown (TCP peer, NOT real client)

content.query:
ApiManagementGatewayLogs
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where isnotempty(RequestHeaders)
| extend headers = parse_json(RequestHeaders)
| extend xff = tostring(headers["X-Forwarded-For"])
| extend OriginalClientIp = trim(' ', tostring(split(xff, ',')[0]))
| project TimeGenerated, CallerIpAddress, XffHeader = xff, OriginalClientIp, Method, Url, ResponseCode, ApiId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
content.title: APIM — Requests with XFF Header (real client IP extraction)

content.query:
ApiManagementGatewayLogs
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize RequestCount = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), ApiId
| render timechart
content.title: APIM — Request Volume Over Time by API

content.query:
AppRequests
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where AppRoleName has 'apim' or SDKVersion has 'apim' or Properties has 'apim'
| extend xff = tostring(Properties["Request-Header-x-forwarded-for"])
| summarize
    TotalRequests = count(),
    WithXFF = countif(isnotempty(xff)),
    WithoutXFF = countif(isempty(xff))
| extend XffPresencePct = iff(TotalRequests > 0, round(100.0 * WithXFF / TotalRequests, 2), 0.0)
| project TotalRequests, WithXFF, WithoutXFF, XffPresencePct
content.title: APIM — XFF Presence Rate via App Insights

content.json:
---
## 4. Side-by-Side — What Each Tier Sees

> This is the **key demo visual**. Same requests, different IPs at each tier.

content.query:
let agw = AzureDiagnostics
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where ResourceType == 'APPLICATIONGATEWAYS'
| summarize Count = count() by IP = clientIP_s
| extend Tier = 'App Gateway (clientIP_s)', Note = case(
    IP == '4.174.181.251', 'Proxy VIP ← NOT real client',
    IP == '4.205.85.213', 'Self (health probe)',
    'Direct caller');
let apim = ApiManagementGatewayLogs
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize Count = count() by IP = CallerIpAddress
| extend Tier = 'APIM (CallerIpAddress)', Note = case(
    IP == '4.174.181.251', 'Proxy VIP ← NOT real client',
    IP == '4.205.85.213', 'App GW IP ← NOT real client',
    'Direct caller');
let appsvc = AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize Count = count() by IP = CIp
| extend Tier = 'App Service (CIp)', Note = case(
    IP == '4.205.85.213', 'App GW IP ← NOT real client',
    'Direct caller');
union agw, apim, appsvc
| project Tier, IP, Count, Note
| order by Tier asc, Count desc
content.title: Cross-Tier IP Comparison — Same Requests, Different IPs

content.json:
---
## 5. The Solution — Where to Find the Real Client IP

> When `ForwardedHeadersMiddleware` + `XffTelemetryInitializer` are deployed, the real client IP appears in **Application Insights `customDimensions["X-Forwarded-For"]`**.
> 
> For APIM, configure **Diagnostics Settings** to log `X-Forwarded-For` in `Headers to log` and inspect `ApiManagementGatewayLogs.RequestHeaders`.

content.query:
AppRequests
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| extend xff = tostring(Properties["X-Forwarded-For"])
| extend resolvedIp = tostring(Properties["ResolvedClientIp"])
| where isnotempty(xff)
| extend OriginalClientIp = trim(' ', tostring(split(xff, ',')[0]))
| project TimeGenerated, OriginalClientIp, FullXffChain = xff, ResolvedByMiddleware = resolvedIp, AppInsightsClientIP = ClientIP, Name
| order by TimeGenerated desc
content.title: Application Insights — Real Client IP from XFF (requires middleware)

content.query:
AppRequests
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| extend xff = tostring(Properties["X-Forwarded-For"])
| summarize
    TotalRequests = count(),
    WithXFF = countif(isnotempty(xff)),
    WithoutXFF = countif(isempty(xff))
| extend XffCoveragePct = iff(TotalRequests > 0, round(100.0 * WithXFF / TotalRequests, 1), 0.0)
| project TotalRequests, WithXFF, WithoutXFF, XffCoveragePct
content.title: XFF Coverage — % of Requests with X-Forwarded-For in App Insights

content.json:
---
## 6. Custom Dimensions Explorer

> Explore all available custom dimensions in Application Insights requests to discover XFF-related telemetry.

content.query:
AppRequests
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| project Properties
| mv-apply kv = bag_keys(Properties) on (
    extend Key = tostring(kv), Value = tostring(Properties[tostring(kv)])
)
| where Key has_any ('forward', 'xff', 'client', 'ip', 'x-forwarded', 'Request-Header', 'Resolved')
| summarize SampleValue = take_any(Value), Count = count() by Key
| order by Count desc
content.title: Custom Dimensions — XFF & IP-Related Keys Found in App Insights

content.query:
AppRequests
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| project Properties
| mv-apply kv = bag_keys(Properties) on (
    extend Key = tostring(kv)
)
| summarize Count = count() by Key
| order by Count desc
| take 30
content.title: All Custom Dimension Keys (Top 30)

content.json:
---
## 7. Summary — Proof of Issue

| Finding | Evidence |
|---------|----------|
| **App Gateway `clientIP_s`** shows Proxy VIP, not real client | See Section 1 — IP `4.174.181.251` is the ACI proxy |
| **APIM `CallerIpAddress`** shows App GW or Proxy IP, not real client | See Section 3 — XFF header must be inspected |
| **App Service `CIp`** shows App Gateway IP, not real client | See Section 2 — IP `4.205.85.213` is the App GW |
| **Real client IP** only available in `X-Forwarded-For` header | See Section 5 — requires ForwardedHeaders middleware |

### Fix Steps
1. **Proxy (VIP):** Must set `X-Forwarded-For` header (Nginx: `proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for`)
2. **App Gateway:** Deploy XFF rewrite rule with `{var_add_x_forwarded_for_proxy}` to strip `:port`
3. **APIM:** Configure diagnostic settings to log `X-Forwarded-For` header; use policy to forward XFF to backend
4. **App Service:** Deploy `ForwardedHeadersMiddleware` + `XffTelemetryInitializer` to capture XFF in App Insights

> ⚠️ **Clean up when done:** `az group delete -n rg-xff-test-eastus --yes --no-wait`
